verification.description: Codebook utilization: count unique s1+s2 token IDs across all bars
verification.command: python -m src.core.kronos.tokenizer --input data/v3/processed --output data/v3/tokens --quality-check

verification.description: Token entropy: information-theoretic diversity of token distribution
verification.command: python -m src.core.kronos.tokenizer --input data/v3/processed --output data/v3/tokens --quality-check

verification.description: Reconstruction MSE: fidelity of OHLCV round-trip through VQ-VAE
verification.command: python -m src.core.kronos.tokenizer --input data/v3/processed --output data/v3/tokens --quality-check
verification.note: If > 0.05: reduce codebook bits, may indicate domain shift. If 0.01-0.05: proceed with caution, monitor Phase 2 metrics.

verification.description: Per-asset minimum unique codes: ensure no ticker collapses to < 50 codes
verification.command: python -m src.core.kronos.tokenizer --input data/v3/processed --output data/v3/tokens --quality-check
verification.note: If any asset < 20: remove from universe. If 20-50: flag, may be low-volatility asset.

verification.description: Cross-sectional RMS correlation: assets must be differentiable
verification.command: python -m src.core.kronos.tokenizer --input data/v3/processed --output data/v3/tokens --quality-check
verification.note: If > 0.7: apply sector-neutral z-scoring. All assets moving together → no cross-sectional spread.

verification.description: No NaN in token output — all (B,T) arrays are valid int64
verification.command: python -c 'import numpy as np; d=np.load("data/v3/tokens/SBER_train.npy"); assert not np.isnan(d).any()'

verification.description: Tokenizer frozen mode: requires_grad = False for all tokenizer params
verification.command: python -c 'from src.core.kronos.tokenizer import KronosTokenizer; t=KronosTokenizer.from_pretrained("NeoQuasar/Kronos-Tokenizer-2k"); assert all(not p.requires_grad for p in t.parameters())'

verification.description: CE loss convergence: validation CE monotonically decreases over 30 epochs
verification.command: python src/core/kronos/fine_tune.py --resume kronos_moex_best.pt; grep val_ce checkpoints/kronos_moex/history.json
verification.expected: Val CE at epoch 30 < Val CE at epoch 5 (decreasing trend)

verification.description: Train CE vs Val CE gap: check for overfitting
verification.command: python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('checkpoints/kronos_moex/history.json')); gap=abs(d['train_ce'][-1]-d['val_ce'][-1]); print(gap)"
verification.expected: Train CE - Val CE gap < 0.5 (not overfitting). If gap > 0.5: add dropout 0.2

verification.description: Val CE vs random baseline: model must beat random token prediction
verification.command: python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('checkpoints/kronos_moex/history.json')); ce=d['val_ce'][-1]; random_ce=10.0; assert ce < 0.9 * random_ce, f'CE {ce} >= {0.9*random_ce}'"
verification.note: If val CE >= random CE: model not learning — check tokenizer, data quality.

verification.description: No NaN in loss: verify all gradients are finite
verification.command: python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/kronos_moex_best.pt', map_location='cpu'); assert not any(torch.isnan(p).any() for p in ckpt.values())"
verification.expected: All parameters finite, no NaN in checkpoint

verification.command: tail -100 logs/fine_tune.log | grep 'grad_norm'

verification.description: Checkpoint resume integrity: resume produces same loss trajectory
verification.command: diff <(python fine_tune.py --epochs 5) <(python fine_tune.py --epochs 3; python fine_tune.py --resume --epochs 2) | grep val_ce
verification.expected: Resumed run matches fresh run val_CE within 1e-6 tolerance

verification.description: Per-asset CE tracking: no single asset dominates loss improvement
verification.command: grep 'per_asset_ce' checkpoints/kronos_moex/history.json
verification.expected: Std dev of per-asset val CE < 2.0 (all assets learning)
verification.note: If one asset dominates: possible data quality issue with that ticker.

verification.description: Sharpe ratio: annualized risk-adjusted return on test set
verification.command: python -m src.evaluation.backtest --predictions data/v3/predictions --split test; cat data/v3/results/report.json | jq .sharpe
verification.note: If < 0.0: go to Phase 3 iteration. If 0.0-0.5: weak but positive.

verification.description: Win rate: percentage of profitable trades
verification.command: cat data/v3/results/report.json | jq .win_rate
cat data/v3/results/report.json | jq .max_dd

verification.description: PSR (Probabilistic Sharpe Ratio): statistical significance of Sharpe
verification.command: cat data/v3/results/report.json | jq .psr
verification.note: If < 0.3: Sharpe not robust — increase MC sample_count.

verification.description: Cross-sectional IC mean: information coefficient across assets per timestamp
verification.command: cat data/v3/results/report.json | jq .ic_mean

verification.description: Walk-forward consistency: val split metrics within 20% of test split
verification.command: python -m src.evaluation.backtest --split val; python -m src.evaluation.backtest --split test; python -c 'compare_val_test()'

verification.description: Session enforcement: all entries between 10:00-18:10, all exits by 18:35
verification.command: python -c "import json; t=json.load(open('data/v3/results/report.json')); assert t['session_violations'] == 0"

verification.description: No duplicate trades: each (timestamp, ticker) pair has >= 1 entry
verification.command: python -c "import pandas as pd; df=pd.read_parquet('data/v3/results/trades.parquet'); assert df.duplicated(['pred_ts','ticker']).sum() == 0"

verification.description: Temporal split: train indices < val indices < test indices
verification.command: python -c "assert train_end_date < val_start_date; assert val_end_date < test_start_date"

verification.description: Feature leakage: no future data in lookback window
verification.command: python -c "x_df=candles[t:t+506]; y_df=candles[t+506:t+512]; assert x_df.index.max() < y_df.index.min()"
verification.expected: All lookback bars strictly before first prediction bar

verification.description: Per-window z-score: mu,sigma computed per individual window (no global stats)
verification.command: python -c "for w in windows: mu=np.mean(w); sigma=np.std(w); assert mu and sigma computed per-window"
verification.expected: No global z-score normalization — each window independently normalized

verification.command: python -c "assert step == pred_len == 6; for i in range(1, len(windows)): assert windows[i].start == windows[i-1].end"
verification.expected: Windows are contiguous/non-overlapping with no gap or overlap

verification.description: Session filter: only main session 10:00-18:40 MSK bars present
verification.command: python -c "assert all(d.hour*60+d.minute >= 600 for d in df.index); assert all(d.hour*60+d.minute <= 1120 for d in df.index)"
verification.expected: No bars outside 10:00-18:40 MSK (no overnight, pre-market, post-market)

verification.description: Consensus leakage: consensus computed from sample predictions only, never actuals
verification.command: python -c "assert 'actual' not in consensus_fn.__code__.co_varnames; assert all(k in ['close_samples','prev_close','threshold'] for k in consensus_fn.__code__.co_varnames)"
verification.expected: consensus() function signature has no 'actual' parameter

verification.description: TP/SL no lookahead: exit decision uses only current bar high/low, not future
verification.command: python -c "assert 'actuals[i,0]' in trade for i in range(pred_len); assert 'actuals[j,1]' not in trade for j > i"
verification.expected: Trade exit loop checks actuals[i].high/low, never actuals[i+1]

verification.description: Cross-sectional z-score: ranking uses same-timestamp predictions only
verification.command: python -c "for t in timestamps: preds=get_preds_at(t); assert all(p.timestamp == t for p in preds); zscores=(preds-mean(preds))/std(preds)"
verification.expected: All z-scores computed cross-sectionally at identical timestamps

verification.description: GRACE lint: autonomous profile passes without errors
verification.command: grace lint --profile autonomous docs/grace/

verification.description: All 6 GRACE artifacts present and well-formed XML
verification.command: find docs/grace/ -name '*.xml' | wc -l ; for f in docs/grace/*.xml; do xmllint --noout "$f" 2>&1; done
verification.expected: 6 files found, xmllint passes for all

verification.description: AGENTS.md grep mappings resolve to existing files
verification.command: for mapping in docs/grace/ docs/module-contracts.md config/global.yaml; do test -e "$mapping" && echo 'OK: '$mapping; done
verification.expected: All 6 grep targets in AGENTS.md resolve to existing files

verification.description: No stale M-XXX module references (all IDs in development-plan match AGENTS.md)
verification.command: diff <(grep -oP 'M-[A-Z0-9-]+' AGENTS.md | sort -u) <(grep -oP 'M-[A-Z0-9-]+' docs/grace/development-plan.xml | sort -u)
verification.expected: No orphan M-XXX IDs that exist in one file but not the other

verification.command: python -m src.data.fetcher --help
verification.expected: Prints usage, all flags, exit codes 0

verification.command: python -m src.data.fetcher --dry-run --start 2023-01-01 --end 2023-02-01
verification.expected: Prints plan: N tickers × M months, no data fetched

verification.command: python -m src.data.fetcher --status
verification.expected: Prints manifest status or 'no manifest found', exit code 0

verification.command: python -m src.core.kronos.predictor --help
python -m src.core.kronos.tokenizer --help

verification.description: Manifest validation chain: each module validates predecessor manifest
verification.command: python -c "from src.data.fetcher import ManifestValidator; assert hasattr(ManifestValidator, 'validate')"
verification.expected: Each module has manifest validation before execution, exits with code 1 on failure

verification_suite.name: Import Integrity
verification_suite.description: Verify all src.* imports resolve without circular dependencies.

verification.command: python -c "from src.core.kronos.modules import HierarchicalEmbedding, TransformerBlock, RMSNorm, DualHead, DifferentiableEntropyFunction, BSQuantizer"
python -c "from src.data.fetcher import fetch_and_save; from src.data.base import DataSource; from src.data.cache import DataCache"

python -c "from src.signals.atoms import consensus, boundaries, dispersion; from src.signals.bollinger import bb_signal, compute_bb; from src.signals.ict import detect_order_block, detect_fvg; from src.signals.volatility import compute_atr; from src.signals.vwap import compute_vwap; from src.signals.fractal import find_fractals; from src.signals.divergence import rsi; from src.signals.bars import classify_bar"

python -c "from src.strategies.core import load_config, _simulate_trade, report; from src.strategies.vanilla import run; from src.strategies.s01_bb import run; from src.strategies.s02_bb_mr import run; from src.strategies.s05_bb_breakout import run; from src.strategies.s20_ob import run; from src.strategies.s28_vol_ob import run; from src.strategies.s34_vwap_ob import run; from src.strategies.s38_lowvol_ob import run"

python -c "from src.evaluation.metrics import sharpe_ratio, max_drawdown, win_rate, psr, ic_rank, StrategyMetrics; from src.evaluation.evaluate import evaluate; from src.evaluation.output import save_samples, reconstruct; from src.evaluation.walk_forward import run_walk_forward; from src.evaluation.calibrate import run_calibration"
python -c "import importlib; import sys; modules=sorted([m for m in sys.modules if m.startswith('src.')]); print('\\n'.join(modules))" 2>&1 | head -50
verification.expected: Linear dependency: data → kronos → signals → strategies → evaluation. No cycles.

verification.description: All signal atoms import and produce expected output shapes
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.atoms import direction, consensus, boundaries, dispersion, trend_strength, linearity, asymmetry, belief_weight, expectancy; import numpy as np; x=np.random.randn(5,12).astype(np.float32); prev=100.0; assert direction(x,prev)['dir_sign'].shape==(5,12); assert consensus(x,prev)['has_consensus'].shape==(12,); assert boundaries(x,prev)['tp_long'].shape==(12,); assert dispersion(x,prev)['std_return'].shape==(12,); assert trend_strength(x,prev)['trend_strength']>=0; assert linearity(x)['mean_r2']>=0; assert asymmetry(x,prev)['asymmetry_ratio']>=0; b=np.random.rand(5,12,4); assert belief_weight(b)['belief_score']>=0; assert expectancy(x,prev)['sharpe_avg']!=0; print('OK')"
verification.expected: All 9 atoms execute without error, exit code 0

verification.description: Consensus leakage: consensus() uses only samples + prev_close, never actuals
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.atoms import consensus; import inspect; sig=inspect.signature(consensus); params=list(sig.parameters.keys()); assert 'close_samples' in params; assert 'prev_close' in params; assert 'actual' not in params; print('OK')"

verification.description: Bar classification and signal generation import and run
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.bars import classify_bar, compute_bar_probs, bar_signal, ALL_TYPES; import numpy as np; o=np.array([100,101,102],dtype=float); h=np.array([102,103,104],dtype=float); l=np.array([99,100,101],dtype=float); c=np.array([101,102,103],dtype=float); bt=classify_bar(101,103,100,102,100,102,99,101); assert bt in ALL_TYPES; probs=compute_bar_probs(o,h,l,c,lookback=100); assert all(t in probs for t in ALL_TYPES); sig=bar_signal(o,h,l,c,probs); assert sig['signal'] in ('bullish','bearish','none'); print('OK')"
verification.expected: All imports resolve, bar classification returns valid type, signal generation returns valid signal

verification.description: BB computation, position, squeeze, and signal all produce valid output
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.bollinger import compute_bb, bb_position, bb_squeeze, bb_signal; import numpy as np; c=np.random.randn(100).cumsum()+100; bb=compute_bb(c); assert 'sma' in bb and 'upper' in bb and 'lower' in bb; pos=bb_position(c[-1],bb['last']['upper'],bb['last']['lower'],bb['last']['sma']); assert pos['zone'] in ('above_upper','below_lower','near_upper','near_lower','middle','unknown'); sq=bb_squeeze(bb['width']); assert 'is_squeeze' in sq; sig=bb_signal(c); assert sig['mr_long'] in (True,False) and sig['mr_short'] in (True,False); print('OK')"
verification.expected: All 4 BB functions execute without error, exit code 0

verification.description: RSI, OBV, MFI, and divergence detection import and produce valid output
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.divergence import rsi, obv_data, mfi, detect_divergence, compute_all; import numpy as np; c=np.random.randn(100).cumsum()+100; v=np.random.rand(100)*1e6; h=c+np.random.rand(100); l=c-np.random.rand(100); r=rsi(c); assert 'oversold' in r and 'overbought' in r; o=obv_data(c,v); assert 'obv_slope' in o; m=mfi(h,l,c,v); assert 'mfi' in m; d=detect_divergence(c,r['rsi_series']); assert d['div_type'] in ('bearish','bullish','none'); all_r=compute_all(h,l,c,v); assert all_r['div_rsi'] in ('bearish','bullish','none'); print('OK')"
verification.expected: All 5 divergence functions execute without error, exit code 0

verification.description: All filter functions import and basic computation works
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.filters import compute_bb, compute_lr, compute_atr_filter, compute_volume_filter, compute_bb_momentum, compute_conf_trend, compute_mc_breadth, compute_rsi14, compute_pred_z, compute_mc_agreement, compute_weighted_quantiles, compute_best_mc, compute_drop_low_conf, compute_asymmetry_ratio, apply_filters; import numpy as np; N=50; LK=100; PL=12; data={'N':N,'LK':LK,'PL':PL,'close_arr':np.random.rand(N+LK-1)*100+100,'entry_close':np.random.rand(N)*100+100,'entry_open':np.random.rand(N)*100+100,'raw':np.random.rand(N+LK-1,5)*100+100,'conf':np.random.rand(N),'preds':np.random.rand(N,5,PL,4),'pred_ret':np.random.randn(N),'belief':np.random.rand(N,5,PL,4),'g_tp':np.random.rand(N)*100+100,'g_sl':np.random.rand(N)*100+100,'wf_q90':np.random.rand(N),'wf_q10':np.random.rand(N),'wf_signal':np.random.randint(-1,2,size=N)}; bb=compute_bb(data); assert len(bb)==9; lr=compute_lr(data); assert len(lr)==8; sig=np.random.randint(-1,2,size=N); out=apply_filters(sig,data,has_bbpct=True); assert len(out)==N; print('OK')"
verification.expected: All 15 filter functions import and execute without error, exit code 0

verification.description: No NaN in any OHLCV column
verification.command: python -c "import pandas as pd; import glob; for f in glob.glob('data/v3/raw/*.parquet'): df=pd.read_parquet(f); assert not df[['open','high','low','close','volume']].isna().any().any(), f'{f} has NaN'"
verification.expected: Zero NaN values in any ticker file

verification.command: python -c "import pandas as pd; import glob; for f in glob.glob('data/v3/raw/*.parquet'): df=pd.read_parquet(f); assert len(df) >= 500, f'{f} has only {len(df)} bars'"
python -c "import pandas as pd; import glob; for f in glob.glob('data/v3/raw/*.parquet'): df=pd.read_parquet(f); assert df['timestamp'].is_monotonic_increasing, f'{f} timestamps not sorted'"
verification.expected: All timestamps sorted ascending per file

verification.description: Volume > 0 for all bars (zero-volume = holiday or API error)
verification.command: python -c "import pandas as pd; import glob; for f in glob.glob('data/v3/raw/*.parquet'): df=pd.read_parquet(f); zero_vol=len(df[df['volume']<0]); assert zero_vol==0, f'{f} has {zero_vol} negative volume bars'"
verification.expected: No negative volume bars. Zero volume ok (holidays).

verification.description: Amount column computed: close * volume * lot_size (per ticker)
verification.command: python -c "from config import load_global_yaml; cfg=load_global_yaml(); lots=cfg['lot_sizes']; for ticker,lot in lots.items(): df=pd.read_parquet(f'data/v3/raw/{ticker}.parquet'); expected=df['close']*df['volume']*lot; assert (abs(df['amount']-expected).mean() < 1.0) if 'amount' in df.columns else True"
verification.expected: Amount column = close * volume * lot_size within tolerance

verification.command: python -m src.cli.backtest --help
verification.expected: Prints usage with all flags, exit code 0

verification.description: Run WF strategy backtest and produce metrics
verification.command: python -m src.cli.backtest --strategy wf --name test-wf --pl 12 --json
verification.expected: JSON output with sharpe, sortino, max_dd etc.

verification.command: python -m src.cli.backtest --strategy wf --name test-reg --pl 12 --register
python -m src.cli.compare --help
verification.expected: Prints usage with --ref and --test flags, exit code 0

verification.description: Compare two registry strategies with JSON output
verification.command: python -m src.cli.compare --ref 'WF+BB%B+BBmom+rollWR noTP' --test 'WF+BB%B+BBmom+rollWR noTP' --json
verification.expected: JSON output with ref and test metrics

verification.description: Compare strategy vs signals.npy with side-by-side table
verification.command: python -m src.cli.compare --ref 'WF+BB%B+BBmom+rollWR noTP' --test 'WF+BB%B+BBmom+rollWR noTP'
python -m src.strategies.registry --top 5
python -m src.strategies.registry --stats
python -m src.strategies.registry --lookup 'WF+BB%B+BBmom+rollWR noTP'
python -m src.strategies.registry --discover
python -m src.strategies.registry --export-json
python -c 'from src.core.registry import register_model, get_model, list_models, BaseModel'

python -c 'from src.core.registry import register_model, get_model, list_models, BaseModel; class DummyModel(BaseModel): def predict(self,df,pred_len,**kw): import pandas as pd; return pd.DataFrame(); def load(self): pass; register_model("test", DummyModel); m = get_model("test"); assert "test" in list_models(); assert isinstance(m, BaseModel)'
python -c 'from src.core.registry import BaseModel; try: class Bad(BaseModel): pass; Bad(); assert False; except TypeError as e: assert "predict" in str(e)'
python -c 'from src.core.registry import list_models; assert "kronos_mini" in list_models()'
python -c "from src.data.base import DataSource"

verification.description: DataSource ABC cannot be instantiated directly
verification.command: python -c "from src.data.base import DataSource; ds=DataSource(); assert False, 'should raise'" 2>&1 || test $? -eq 0
verification.expected: TypeError: Can't instantiate abstract class DataSource

verification.command: python -c "from src.data.base import DataSource; import abc; methods=set(m for m in DataSource.__abstractmethods__); assert methods=={'fetch_candles','fetch_securities','fetch_index_candles'}, f'Got {methods}'"

verification.description: Fetcher subclass implements all abstract methods
verification.command: python -c "from src.data.fetcher import Fetcher; from src.data.base import DataSource; assert issubclass(Fetcher, DataSource); f=Fetcher(); f.fetch_candles('SBER',10,'2023-01-01','2023-01-02'); f.fetch_securities(); f.fetch_index_candles('IMOEX','2023-01-01','2023-01-02')"
verification.expected: No error — all 3 methods callable on Fetcher instance

verification.command: python -c "from src.data.cache import DataCache"

python -c "import tempfile, pandas as pd; from src.data.cache import DataCache; d=DataCache(tempfile.mkdtemp()); df=pd.DataFrame({'a':[1,2,3]}); d.put('test',df); got=d.get('test'); assert df.equals(got), 'roundtrip failed'; print('OK')"
python -c "import tempfile; from src.data.cache import DataCache; d=DataCache(tempfile.mkdtemp()); assert d.get('nonexistent') is None; print('OK')"

python -c "from src.data.cache import DataCache; k1=DataCache.key('SBER',10,'2023-01-01','2023-01-31'); k2=DataCache.key('SBER',10,'2023-01-01','2023-01-31'); assert k1==k2; assert k1=='SBER_10_2023-01-01_2023-01-31'; print('OK')"

verification.description: Corrupt cache file is removed on read
verification.command: python -c "import tempfile, pathlib; from src.data.cache import DataCache; tmp=tempfile.mkdtemp(); d=DataCache(tmp); d.put('tmp',pd.DataFrame()); p=d._path('tmp'); p.write_text('garbage'); got=d.get('tmp'); assert got is None; assert not p.exists(); print('OK')"
verification.expected: OK — corrupt file removed, None returned

verification.description: Fetcher import resolves with all dependencies
verification.command: python -c "from src.data.fetcher import Fetcher, fetch_and_save, fetch_all, add_amount_column, save_parquets"
python -c "from src.data.fetcher import Fetcher; from src.data.base import DataSource; assert issubclass(Fetcher, DataSource); print('OK')"

verification.description: Month chunking produces correct non-overlapping intervals
verification.command: python -c "from src.data.fetcher import _month_chunks; chunks=_month_chunks('2023-01-01','2023-03-15'); assert len(chunks)==3; assert chunks[0]==('2023-01-01','2023-01-31'); assert chunks[1]==('2023-02-01','2023-02-28'); assert chunks[2]==('2023-03-01','2023-03-15'); print('OK')"
python -c "from src.data.fetcher import _expected_bars; bars=_expected_bars('2024-01-01','2024-01-31'); assert bars > 1000 and bars < 2000; print(f'OK: {bars} bars')"
verification.expected: ~1456 bars for Jan 2024 (52 bars/day * ~28 trading days)

verification.command: python -c "from src.data.fetcher import LOT_SIZES, TICKERS; missing=[t for t in TICKERS if t not in LOT_SIZES]; assert not missing, f'Missing: {missing}'; print(f'OK: {len(LOT_SIZES)} lot sizes')"
verification.expected: OK — all 20 stock tickers have lot sizes

verification.command: python -c "from src.data.loader_sber import load_sber_data, DATA_DIR, RAW_DIR"

verification.description: load_sber returns dict with all 16 required keys
verification.command: python -c "from src.data.loader_sber import load_sber_data; import os; has_files=os.path.exists('data/v3/predictions/10min_sber_mini/SBER_preds_pl12_sc5.npy'); print(f'Has files: {has_files}')"
verification.note: Only runs if prediction files exist on disk

verification.command: python -c "from src.data.loader_sber import LK, PL, COMM, Q_LONG, Q_SHORT, TP_Q, SL_Q; assert LK==500; assert PL==12; assert COMM==0.0; assert Q_LONG==0.9; assert Q_SHORT==0.1; assert TP_Q==0.8; assert SL_Q==0.2; print('OK')"
python -c 'from src.core.kronos.model import Kronos'

verification.description: Kronos model class has expected attributes
verification.command: python -c 'from src.core.kronos.model import Kronos; attrs=("s1_vocab_size","d_model","n_layers","n_heads"); assert all(hasattr(Kronos, a) for a in attrs), [a for a in attrs if not hasattr(Kronos, a)]'
verification.expected: Attributes s1_vocab_size, d_model, n_layers, n_heads exist

verification.description: Kronos model has forward, decode_s1, decode_s2 methods
verification.command: python -c 'from src.core.kronos.model import Kronos; assert hasattr(Kronos, "forward") and hasattr(Kronos, "decode_s1") and hasattr(Kronos, "decode_s2")'
python -c 'from src.core.kronos.modules import RMSNorm; m=RMSNorm(64); assert m.weight.shape[0]==64; import torch; out=m(torch.randn(2,10,64)); assert out.shape==(2,10,64)'
verification.expected: RMSNorm(64) creates weight(64,) and returns (2,10,64) shape

verification.command: python -c 'from src.core.kronos.modules import HierarchicalEmbedding; he=HierarchicalEmbedding(10,10,256); import torch; out=he([torch.zeros(2,512).long(), torch.zeros(2,512).long()]); assert out.shape==(2,512,256)'
python -c 'from src.core.kronos.modules import TransformerBlock; tb=TransformerBlock(256, 4, 1024); assert tb.self_attn.n_heads==4'

python -c 'from src.core.kronos.predictor import top_k_top_p_filtering, sample_from_logits, auto_regressive_inference, auto_regressive_inference_raw, calc_time_stamps, KronosPredictor, KronosModel, load_model'
python -c 'from src.core.kronos.predictor import KronosPredictor; assert hasattr(KronosPredictor, "generate") and hasattr(KronosPredictor, "predict") and hasattr(KronosPredictor, "predict_batch")'

python -c 'from src.core.kronos.predictor import top_k_top_p_filtering; import torch; l=torch.randn(1,1024); f=top_k_top_p_filtering(l, top_k=50); assert f.shape==l.shape; f2=top_k_top_p_filtering(l, top_p=0.9); assert f2.shape==l.shape'
verification.expected: top_k and top_p filtering both return same shape

verification.command: python -c 'from src.core.kronos.predictor import calc_time_stamps; import pandas as pd; ts=pd.Series(pd.date_range("2023-01-01", periods=5, freq="h")); df=calc_time_stamps(ts); assert list(df.columns)==["minute","hour","weekday","day","month"]'
verification.expected: 5 time columns: minute, hour, weekday, day, month

verification.command: python -c 'from src.core.kronos.tokenizer import KronosTokenizer'

verification.description: Tokenizer has encode, decode, indices_to_bits methods
verification.command: python -c 'from src.core.kronos.tokenizer import KronosTokenizer; assert hasattr(KronosTokenizer, "encode") and hasattr(KronosTokenizer, "decode") and hasattr(KronosTokenizer, "indices_to_bits")'

verification.description: Tokenizer __init__ signature accepts s1_bits, s2_bits, d_model
verification.command: python -c 'from src.core.kronos.tokenizer import KronosTokenizer; import inspect; sig=inspect.signature(KronosTokenizer.__init__); params=list(sig.parameters.keys()); assert "s1_bits" in params and "s2_bits" in params and "d_model" in params'
python -c "from src.evaluation.calibrate import load_config, fetch_data, pass_1_pred_len_sweep, pass_2_param_sweep, _random_indices"

verification.description: _random_indices produces correct number of sorted indices
verification.command: python -c "import numpy as np; from src.evaluation.calibrate import _random_indices; idx=_random_indices(10000, 500, 12, 50, 42); assert len(idx)==50; assert all(0<=i<9488 for i in idx); assert sorted(idx)==list(idx); print('OK')"
verification.expected: OK — 50 sorted indices in valid range

verification.command: python -c "from src.evaluation.engine import run_backtest, run_backtest_custom, compute_all_metrics, get_tp_sl, STRAT_METRICS, PRED_METRICS, TRADE_METRICS"
python -c "from src.evaluation.engine import STRAT_METRICS, PRED_METRICS, TRADE_METRICS; assert len(STRAT_METRICS)==12; assert len(PRED_METRICS)==6; assert len(TRADE_METRICS)==4; print('OK')"

python -c "import numpy as np; from src.evaluation.engine import compute_all_metrics; r=np.random.randn(1000)*0.01; e=np.cumprod(1+r); m=compute_all_metrics(r,e,r[:500],r[:500],np.random.randint(-1,2,500)); required={'sharpe','sortino','max_dd','profit_factor','win_rate','calmar','avg_return','total_return','n_trades','n_long','n_short','n_bar_active','psr','dsr','dir_acc','dir_sharpe','return_corr','ic_rank','bias','mae'}; assert required.issubset(m.keys()), f'Missing: {required-m.keys()}'; print(f'OK: {len(m)} metrics')"

verification.description: evaluate() imports and returns dict for valid inputs
verification.command: python -c "from src.evaluation.evaluate import evaluate; import numpy as np, pandas as pd; actual=pd.DataFrame({'close':[100,101,102],'high':[101,102,103],'low':[99,100,101],'open':[100,101,102]}); samples=np.random.randn(5,3,6)*0.01+100; r=evaluate(actual,samples,100.0); assert isinstance(r,dict); assert 'direction_accuracy' in r; assert 'consensus_rate' in r; print(f'OK: {len(r)} metrics')"

verification.description: evaluate() returns empty dict for empty samples
verification.command: python -c "from src.evaluation.evaluate import evaluate; import numpy as np, pandas as pd; actual=pd.DataFrame({'close':[100],'high':[101],'low':[99],'open':[100]}); r=evaluate(actual,np.array([]),100.0); assert r=={}; print('OK')"
verification.expected: OK — empty dict for empty samples

verification.command: python -c "from src.evaluation.metrics import sharpe_ratio, max_drawdown, profit_factor, win_rate, calmar_ratio, direction_accuracy, direction_sharpe, return_correlation, ic_rank, bias, mae, prediction_volatility, psr, dsharpe_ratio, sortino_ratio, avg_return, n_trades, trade_pct, StrategyMetrics, evaluate_model"
python -c "import numpy as np; from src.evaluation.metrics import sharpe_ratio; r=np.ones(100)*0.001; sr=sharpe_ratio(r); assert abs(sr)<1e-6, f'Expected 0 got {sr}'; print('OK')"
python -c "import numpy as np; from src.evaluation.metrics import max_drawdown; e=np.exp(np.linspace(0,0.1,100)); dd=max_drawdown(e); assert abs(dd)<1e-6; print('OK')"
verification.expected: OK — no drawdown on monotonic equity

verification.command: python -c "import numpy as np; from src.evaluation.metrics import psr; r=np.random.randn(1000)*0.01+0.001; p=psr(r); assert 0<=p<=1; assert p>0.5, f'Expected >0.5 got {p}'; print(f'OK: PSR={p:.4f}')"

python -c "import numpy as np; from src.evaluation.metrics import StrategyMetrics; m=StrategyMetrics(name='test',total_trades=100,win_rate=0.6,sharpe_ratio=1.5,max_drawdown=-0.1,total_return=0.2); assert m.name=='test'; assert m.win_rate==0.6; print('OK')"
python -c "from src.evaluation.output import save_samples, load_samples, reconstruct, compute_monthly_metrics, save_monthly_metrics, trade_summary, save_beliefs, load_beliefs, save_summary"

python -c "import numpy as np; from src.evaluation.output import reconstruct; arr=np.random.randn(5,12).astype(np.float32); blob=arr.tobytes(); out=reconstruct(blob,5,12); assert np.allclose(arr,out); print('OK')"
python -c "from src.evaluation.quarterly import compute_quarterly_tables, save_results_csv, save_quarterly_csv, compute_h1_2026_metrics, save_all_results"

python -c "import numpy as np; from src.evaluation.quarterly import compute_quarterly_tables; ts=np.array(['2025-01-15','2025-04-15','2025-07-15','2026-01-15']); labels=['test']; pb=[np.random.randn(4)*0.01]; bars,qtrs=compute_quarterly_tables(labels,pb,ts); assert '2025-Q1' in bars; assert '2025-Q2' in bars; assert '2025-Q3' in bars; assert '2026-Q1' in bars; print(f'OK: {len(bars)} quarters')"

verification.description: Regime module exports all public functions
verification.command: python -c "from src.evaluation.regime import apply_filters, build_regime_masks, champion_breakdown, classify_regimes, compute_bar_metadata, compute_quarterly_breakdown, compute_raw_bb_mapping, top5_15h_16h"

python -c "import numpy as np; from src.evaluation.regime import compute_bar_metadata; ts=np.array(['2025-01-15 10:30','2025-01-15 14:00','2025-01-17 18:00']); hour,dow=compute_bar_metadata(ts); assert list(hour)==[10,14,18]; assert list(dow)==[2,2,4]; print('OK')"

python -c "import numpy as np; from src.evaluation.regime import apply_filters; sig=np.array([1,0,-1,1,0]); data={'bb_pct':np.array([0.1,0.5,0.9,0.2,0.5]),'bb_width':np.array([0.02,0.02,0.02,0.02,0.02]),'wf_width':np.array([0.01,0.01,0.01,0.01,0.01]),'bb_mom':np.array([True,True,True,True,True])}; out=apply_filters(sig,data,has_bbpct=True); assert out[0]==0; assert out[2]==0; assert out[3]==0; print('OK')"
python -c "from src.evaluation.simulation import simulate_trade, get_tp_sl, get_tp_sl_no_sl, get_tp_sl_no_tp, _weighted_q, get_tp_sl_w"

verification.description: simulate_trade returns None for invalid entry
verification.command: python -c "import numpy as np; from src.evaluation.simulation import simulate_trade; raw=np.random.randn(1000,5); bar,ret,hold=simulate_trade(0,1,105,95,np.array([0.0]),raw,500,12); assert bar is None; assert ret==0.0; assert hold==0; print('OK')"

verification.description: get_tp_sl returns correct TP/SL for long signal
verification.command: python -c "import numpy as np; from src.evaluation.simulation import get_tp_sl; ec=np.array([100.0]); g_tp=np.array([105.0]); g_sl=np.array([95.0]); tp,sl=get_tp_sl(0,1,ec,g_tp,g_sl); assert tp==105.0; assert sl==95.0; print('OK')"
python -c "from src.evaluation.walk_forward import load_mamba_data, extract_ticker, filter_main_session, generate_windows, group_windows_by_month, run_batch_inference"

python -c "import numpy as np, pandas as pd; from src.evaluation.walk_forward import generate_windows; df=pd.DataFrame({'open':np.random.randn(2000).cumsum()+100,'high':np.random.randn(2000).cumsum()+102,'low':np.random.randn(2000).cumsum()+98,'close':np.random.randn(2000).cumsum()+100,'volume':np.ones(2000)*1e6}, index=pd.date_range('2025-01-01',periods=2000,freq='10min')); wins=generate_windows(df,500,12,12,'2025-01-01','2025-02-01'); assert len(wins)>0; for i in range(1,len(wins)): assert wins[i]['start_idx'] >= wins[i-1]['start_idx']+12; print(f'OK: {len(wins)} windows, non-overlapping')"
python -m src.evaluation.walk_forward --help
python -c "from src.signals.fractal import find_fractals, fractal_signal, breakout_signal, cluster_signal, compute_ao, ao_fractal_signal"

verification.description: find_fractals returns correct structure with bullish/bearish indices and levels
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.fractal import find_fractals; import numpy as np; h=np.array([10,11,12,11,10,11,12,13,12,11]); l=np.array([8,9,10,9,8,9,10,11,10,9]); f=find_fractals(h,l); assert 'bullish_idx' in f; assert 'bearish_idx' in f; assert 'bullish_levels' in f; assert 'bearish_levels' in f; assert len(f['bullish_idx'])>=1; assert len(f['bearish_idx'])>=1; print('OK')"
verification.expected: OK — fractal dict with all 4 keys, at least 1 of each type

verification.description: breakout_signal detects bullish breakout above bearish fractal
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.fractal import breakout_signal; import numpy as np; h=np.array([100,101,102,101,100,101,102,103,104,105]); l=np.array([98,99,100,99,98,99,100,101,102,103]); c=np.array([101,102,103,102,101,102,103,104,105,106]); sig=breakout_signal(h,l,c); print(f'signal={sig[\"signal\"]}'); assert sig['signal'] in ('bullish','bearish','none'); print('OK')"

verification.description: compute_ao returns correct sign for obvious trends
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.fractal import compute_ao; import numpy as np; h=np.linspace(100,110,40); l=np.linspace(98,108,40); ao=compute_ao(h,l); assert ao>0; print(f'AO={ao:.4f}')"

verification.description: Short input arrays return safe defaults
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.fractal import fractal_signal, breakout_signal, compute_ao; import numpy as np; h=np.array([100,101,102]); l=np.array([98,99,100]); fs=fractal_signal(h,l); assert fs['signal']=='none'; bs=breakout_signal(h,l,np.array([101,102,103])); assert bs['signal']=='none'; ao=compute_ao(h,l); print(f'OK: fs={fs[\"signal\"]}, bs={bs[\"signal\"]}, ao={ao}')"
verification.expected: All return safe defaults for arrays < 5 elements

verification.description: All ICT detection functions import correctly
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.ict import detect_swings, detect_order_block, detect_fvg, detect_fvg_multi, detect_liquidity_sweep, detect_premium_discount, detect_mss, detect_breaker_block, detect_volume_ob, detect_eqh_eql"

verification.description: detect_swings returns correct structure with bullish/bearish indices
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.ict import detect_swings; import numpy as np; h=np.array([10,12,11,13,12,11,12,13,14,13,12,11]); l=np.array([8,10,9,10,9,8,9,10,11,10,9,8]); sw=detect_swings(h,l,3); assert 'bullish_idx' in sw; assert 'bearish_idx' in sw; assert len(sw['bullish_idx'])>=1; print('OK')"
verification.expected: OK — swing dict with all keys, at least 1 swing

verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.ict import detect_order_block; import numpy as np; o=np.array([101,102,101,103,104]); h=np.array([102,103,102,104,105]); l=np.array([100,101,100,102,103]); c=np.array([100,103,101,104,105]); ob=detect_order_block(o,h,l,c); print(f'signal={ob[\"signal\"]}'); assert ob['signal'] in ('bullish','bearish','none'); print('OK')"

python -c "from src.signals.ict import detect_fvg; import numpy as np; h=np.array([100,105,103,102,101]); l=np.array([98,102,99,100,99]); fvg=detect_fvg(h,l); print(f'signal={fvg[\"signal\"]}'); assert fvg['signal'] in ('bullish','bearish','none'); print('OK')"

python -c "from src.signals.ict import detect_premium_discount; import numpy as np; h=np.array([105,106,107,108,109,110,108,106,104,102]); l=np.array([95,96,97,98,99,100,98,96,94,92]); c=np.array([102,103,104,105,106,107,105,103,101,99]); pd=detect_premium_discount(h,l,c); print(f'zone={pd[\"zone\"]}'); assert pd['zone'] in ('premium','discount','none'); print('OK')"

verification.description: Short input arrays return safe defaults
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.ict import detect_swings, detect_order_block, detect_fvg; import numpy as np; h=np.array([100,101]); l=np.array([98,99]); sw=detect_swings(h,l); ob=detect_order_block(np.array([101,102]),h,l,np.array([100,101])); fvg=detect_fvg(h,l); print(f'OK: sw={len(sw[\"bullish_idx\"])}, ob={ob[\"signal\"]}, fvg={fvg[\"signal\"]}')"
verification.expected: All return safe defaults for short arrays

verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.volatility import compute_atr, compute_adr, volatility_regime"

python -c "from src.signals.volatility import compute_atr; import numpy as np; h=np.random.rand(100)*10+100; l=h-np.random.rand(100)*5; c=(h+l)/2; atr=compute_atr(h,l,c); assert 'atr' in atr; assert 'atr_pct' in atr; assert 'low_vol' in atr; assert 'high_vol' in atr; assert 'rising' in atr; assert 'falling' in atr; assert 0<=atr['atr_pct']<=1; print(f'OK: ATR={atr[\"atr\"]:.4f}, pct={atr[\"atr_pct\"]:.2f}')"
verification.expected: OK — ATR dict with all 6 keys, atr_pct in [0,1]

verification.description: compute_adr returns valid daily range diagnostics
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.volatility import compute_adr; import numpy as np; n=200; h=np.random.rand(n)*10+100; l=h-np.random.rand(n)*5; is_start=np.zeros(n,dtype=bool); is_start[::10]=True; adr=compute_adr(h,l,is_start); assert 'adr' in adr; assert 'current_range' in adr; assert 'range_ratio' in adr; assert 'low_range' in adr; assert 'high_range' in adr; print(f'OK: ADR={adr[\"adr\"]:.4f}, ratio={adr[\"range_ratio\"]:.2f}')"
verification.expected: OK — ADR dict with all 7 keys

verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.volatility import volatility_regime; import numpy as np; n=200; h=np.random.rand(n)*10+100; l=h-np.random.rand(n)*5; c=(h+l)/2; is_start=np.zeros(n,dtype=bool); is_start[::10]=True; regime=volatility_regime(h,l,c,is_start); assert regime in ('compression','expansion','neutral'); print(f'OK: regime={regime}')"

verification.description: Short input arrays return safe defaults
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.volatility import compute_atr, compute_adr; import numpy as np; h=np.array([100,101]); l=np.array([98,99]); c=np.array([99,100]); atr=compute_atr(h,l,c,14); assert atr['atr']==0; is_start=np.array([True,False]); adr=compute_adr(h,l,is_start); assert adr['adr']==0; print('OK')"
verification.expected: All return safe defaults for arrays < period+1

verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.vwap import compute_vwap, anchored_vwap"

verification.description: compute_vwap returns dict with all band keys
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.vwap import compute_vwap; import numpy as np; n=100; h=np.linspace(100,110,n)+np.random.rand(n)*2; l=np.linspace(98,108,n)-np.random.rand(n)*2; c=(h+l)/2; v=np.ones(n)*1e6; is_start=np.zeros(n,dtype=bool); is_start[0]=True; vw=compute_vwap(h,l,c,v,is_start); assert 'vwap' in vw; assert 'upper' in vw; assert 'lower' in vw; assert 'sigma' in vw; assert 'zscore' in vw; assert 'below_vwap' in vw; assert 'above_vwap' in vw; assert 'crossed_up' in vw; assert 'crossed_down' in vw; print(f'OK: vwap={vw[\"vwap\"]:.2f}, z={vw[\"zscore\"]:.2f}')"

verification.description: VWAP cross detection flags direction changes
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.vwap import compute_vwap; import numpy as np; n=50; h=np.ones(n)*101; l=np.ones(n)*99; c=np.concatenate([np.ones(25)*98,np.ones(25)*102]); v=np.ones(n)*1e6; is_start=np.zeros(n,dtype=bool); is_start[0]=True; vw=compute_vwap(h,l,c,v,is_start); assert isinstance(vw['crossed_up'], bool); assert isinstance(vw['crossed_down'], bool); print(f'OK: up={vw[\"crossed_up\"]}, down={vw[\"crossed_down\"]}')"

verification.description: anchored_vwap computes VWAP from arbitrary anchor point
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.vwap import anchored_vwap, compute_vwap; import numpy as np; n=50; h=np.linspace(100,110,n); l=np.linspace(98,108,n); c=(h+l)/2; v=np.ones(n)*1e6; av=anchored_vwap(h,l,c,v,anchor_idx=10); assert 'vwap' in av; print(f'OK: anchored_vwap={av[\"vwap\"]:.2f}')"

verification.description: Short input arrays return safe defaults
verification.command: python -c "from src.signals.vwap import compute_vwap; import numpy as np; h=np.array([100]); l=np.array([98]); c=np.array([99]); v=np.array([1e6]); is_start=np.array([True]); vw=compute_vwap(h,l,c,v,is_start); assert vw['vwap']==99.0; print('OK')"
verification.expected: OK — VWAP equals close for single bar

verification.command: python -c 'from src.strategies.verified.s01_wf import run'

verification.description: WF strategy appears in registry discovery
verification.command: python -m src.strategies.registry --discover
python -c 'from src.strategies.verified.s02_bb_pct import run'
python -m src.strategies.registry --discover
python -c 'from src.strategies.verified.s03_bb_mom import run'
python -m src.strategies.registry --discover
python -c 'from src.strategies.verified.s04_bb_rollwr import run'
python -m src.strategies.registry --discover
